subject: XSS prevention via output encoding
truth: Stop inserting untrusted data into HTML document contexts without proper output encoding; use context-specific encoding (HTML entity, attribute, JavaScript, CSS, URL) for every insertion point.
quote: All variables must be encoded before use
source: OWASP CS: Cross Site Scripting Prevention Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: HTML entity encoding for HTML element context
truth: When inserting untrusted data into the HTML body (between tags), HTML entity encode the data using a library that escapes &, <, >, ", and ' characters.
quote: HTML Entity Encoding
source: OWASP CS: Cross Site Scripting Prevention Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Attribute encoding for HTML attribute context
truth: When inserting untrusted data into HTML attributes, encode all characters except alphanumeric characters with ASCII values less than 256 using &#xHH; format (HH = hex value) and never use untrusted data in href/src/style/event handler attributes.
quote: Except for alphanumeric characters, escape all characters with ASCII values less than 256 with the &#xHH; format
source: OWASP CS: Cross Site Scripting Prevention Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: innerHTML/outerHTML/document.write/writeln with untrusted data
truth: Stop populating innerHTML, outerHTML, document.write, or document.writeln with untrusted data; use safe DOM methods like document.createElement, element.setAttribute, and element.appendChild instead.
quote: Avoid populating the following methods with untrusted data. 1. element.innerHTML = "..."; 2. element.outerHTML = "..."; 3. document.write(...); 4. document.writeln(...);
source: OWASP CS: DOM based XSS Prevention Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: GraphQL introspection
truth: Disable GraphQL introspection in production environments to prevent schema disclosure.
quote: Disable GraphQL introspection in production environments
source: OWASP CS: GraphQL Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: GraphQL query depth limiting
truth: Limit query depth to prevent deeply nested malicious queries from causing denial of service.
quote: Limit query depth
source: OWASP CS: GraphQL Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: GraphQL query complexity/rate limiting
truth: Implement query complexity analysis and rate limiting to prevent resource exhaustion attacks.
quote: Implement query complexity analysis and rate limiting
source: OWASP CS: GraphQL Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: GraphQL authorization per resolver
truth: Enforce authorization checks at the resolver level, not just at the endpoint level, to prevent unauthorized data access.
quote: Enforce authorization checks at the resolver level
source: OWASP CS: GraphQL Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: JWT algorithm confusion
truth: Always validate the JWT algorithm against an expected allowlist; never trust the 'alg' header from the token.
quote: Always validate the JWT algorithm against an expected allowlist
source: OWASP CS: JSON Web Token Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: JWT 'none' algorithm
truth: Reject tokens with 'alg' set to 'none' or any falsy algorithm; do not allow unsigned JWTs.
quote: Reject tokens with 'alg' set to 'none' or any falsy algorithm
source: OWASP CS: JSON Web Token Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: JWT secret key strength
truth: Use a strong, high-entropy secret key for HMAC-based JWTs; do not use weak or guessable secrets.
quote: Use a strong, high-entropy secret key for HMAC-based JWTs
source: OWASP CS: JSON Web Token Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: JWT expiration validation
truth: Always validate the 'exp' (expiration) claim and reject expired tokens.
quote: Always validate the 'exp' (expiration) claim and reject expired tokens
source: OWASP CS: JSON Web Token Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: logging authentication failures at WARN level
truth: Log authentication failures at WARN level, not INFO or higher.
quote: Level: WARN
source: OWASP CS: Application Logging Vocabulary Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: logging password change failures at CRITICAL level
truth: Log password change failures at CRITICAL level, not WARN or INFO.
quote: Level: CRITICAL
source: OWASP CS: Application Logging Vocabulary Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: logging impossible travel events at CRITICAL level
truth: Log impossible travel (potential account takeover) events at CRITICAL level.
quote: Level:: CRITICAL
source: OWASP CS: Application Logging Vocabulary Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: logging authorization failures at CRITICAL level
truth: Log authorization failures at CRITICAL level, not WARN or INFO.
quote: Level:: CRITICAL
source: OWASP CS: Application Logging Vocabulary Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Clear-Site-Data header on session termination
truth: Return the Clear-Site-Data response header during logout or session termination to delete cached resources, cookies, and storage.
quote: applications should ensure that previously stored sensitive data is removed when a session ends. This can be achieved by returning the Clear-Site-Data response header (for example, Clear-Site-Data: "cache", "cookies", "storage") during logout or session termination.
source: OWASP CS: Session Management Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Symfony SQL injection via raw queries
truth: Stop using raw SQL queries with string interpolation; use Doctrine ORM or prepared statements with parameterized queries instead.
quote: raw SQL queries can lead to SQL injection vulnerabilities
source: OWASP CS: Symfony Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Symfony CSRF token validation
truth: Always include and validate CSRF tokens in all forms; Symfony's Form component does this automatically when enabled.
quote: CSRF tokens should be included and validated in all forms
source: OWASP CS: Symfony Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Symfony missing access control checks
truth: Always use Symfony's security voters or @IsGranted annotations to enforce authorization on every controller action.
quote: access control checks should be enforced on every controller action
source: OWASP CS: Symfony Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA

subject: Symfony mass assignment via form binding
truth: Use form classes with explicit field whitelisting instead of binding request data directly to entities to prevent mass assignment.
quote: mass assignment vulnerabilities can occur when binding request data directly to entities
source: OWASP CS: Symfony Cheat Sheet
license_note: OWASP Cheat Sheet Series, CC BY-SA
